questions.question: A thread is executing a loop that iterates 'n' times. The loop performs a critical section of code that is accessed by other threads. What is the primary reason why this is a problem?
questions.hint: Explain the concept of a critical section and its impact on multithreaded programs.

questions.question: Consider a parallel computing program with multiple threads, each executing the same loop. The loop iterates over an array of 'n' elements. If each thread executes the loop independently, what will be the final value of the array?
questions.hint: Explain the concept of parallel execution and its impact on data consistency in multithreaded programs.

questions.question: In a multithreaded program, a thread is executing a loop that accesses a shared variable. The loop iterates 'n' times, and each iteration increments the variable by 1. What will be the final value of the variable after the loop completes?
questions.hint: Explain the concept of atomic operations and their impact on shared variables in multithreaded programs.
